guide_description: One day tracing Prague's historic spine from the castle hill down through the medieval bridge to the Old Town square. The route descends gradually from Hradcany through Mala Strana and across the Vltava, ending in the baroque and Gothic core of Stare Mesto.

days.cluster_tagline: The full Prague descent - hilltop fortress, medieval bridge, and a square unchanged since the 15th century
days.neighborhood_description: Prague's essential corridor runs from Prague Castle on the western hill down through the Little Quarter's baroque palaces and gardens, across the Charles Bridge over the Vltava, and into the Old Town. The entire route sits inside the UNESCO historic zone and can be walked in a single continuous descent.

morning.activity_title: Prague Castle and St. Vitus Cathedral
morning.notes: Enter through the western Hradcanske namesti gate rather than the eastern gate off Jirska - the western approach gives you the full forecourt with the Giant Battling Titans statues and the changing of the guard at the top of the hour. St. Vitus Cathedral is inside the second courtyard and free to enter the nave; the circuit ticket is only needed if you want the treasury, tower, or crypt. The rose window on the south facade is 20th-century but was designed directly from the medieval drawings.

afternoon.notes: The 30 baroque statues lining the bridge are mostly 18th-century replacements; the originals are in the Lapidarium at Vystaviste. The most-touched figure is St. John of Nepomuk (fifth from the Mala Strana end on the north side) - the bronze plaque at his feet is polished bright by centuries of hands. Midday crowds are dense; if you walked down from the castle already, you will cross at exactly the right time before the afternoon tour buses arrive.

evening.activity_title: Old Town Square and the Astronomical Clock
evening.notes: The Astronomical Clock (Orloj) on the south face of the Old Town Hall chimes at the top of every hour and deploys its parade of apostles - the mechanism dates to 1410 and the hourly show is brief but the clock face itself rewards long study. For the best view of the square's full arc of Gothic and baroque facades, climb the Old Town Hall tower rather than standing at ground level. After dark the square empties significantly and the twin spires of Tyn Church are dramatically lit from below.

if_time_permits.notes: A formal 17th-century baroque garden wedged between Prague Castle and the Charles Bridge descent route - directly on the natural walking line between the two. The bronze statues along the central sala terrena are originals; the copies in the garden replaced them after Swedish troops carried the originals off as war booty in 1648. Free entry, and almost always quieter than anything else on the castle circuit.

One of the oldest and most densely layered Jewish cemeteries in Europe, with over 12,000 visible headstones stacked in some places twelve layers deep due to lack of space. Entry requires a combined ticket covering multiple synagogues in the quarter; buy it at the Pinkas Synagogue entrance on Siroka street rather than at the cemetery gate where lines form. A ten-minute walk north from Old Town Square.
